title: W^flip on morphisms (mechanical follow-on to Phase-2.A object construction)
parallel_sprints: Phase-2.B.2 (substantive Bridge Theorem 6.4'-Q1' with off-orbit super-additivity, 2-4 weeks)
downstream_sprints: Phase-2.B.3 (numerical verification panel, 1 week, consumes B.1 morphism specification)
headline_sentence: W^flip on morphisms defined cleanly via Connes-vS contravariant UCP convention (Def 3.1-B1); functoriality verified at theorem-grade rigor (Theorem 1.1-B1); restriction to commutative MS via iota matches A.3' Wick-rotation functor morphism action bit-exact; compresses to single sprint memo per Phase-2.A compression-pattern prediction.
compression_pattern: single sprint memo (mechanical confirmed per Phase-2.A section 7.4 prediction)

source_morphism_class.definition: Definition 2.1-B1 (Topographic Krein M-isometry on enlarged-flip substrate)
source_morphism_class.extends: Phase A.2' Def 2.3 with enlarged topography and explicit cover preservation

axioms.S4_cover_preservation_enlarged: pi(M^L_flip_full_1_k) subseteq M^L_flip_full_2_k for all k

category_structure.identity: id on ambient algebra trivially satisfies (S1)-(S4)

bridge_functor_morphism_action.definition: Definition 3.1-B1: W^flip(pi^K_flip) := (pi^K_flip)^* = algebraic pullback in contravariant direction
bridge_functor_morphism_action.convention: Connes-vS contravariant UCP convention (covariant source -> contravariant target via Gelfand-duality-flavored bridge)

M1_topography_preservation.proof: Direct from source (S2) read in contravariant direction

M2_lipschitz_compatibility.proof: Direct from source (S1) read in contravariant direction

M3_basepoint_state_pullback.proof: Direct from source (S3) read in contravariant direction

M4_cover_preservation.proof: Direct from source (S4) at each cutoff k

M5_modular_flow_intertwining.proof: Automatic from M3 per Phase-2.A Lemma 4.2-OS (Tomita-Takesaki functoriality)

axiom_a_morphism_class_image.description: All five OSLPLS morphism axioms (M1)-(M5) verify on image

axiom_b_identity_preservation.description: W^flip(id) = id by direct computation; identity *-homomorphism is self-pullback

axiom_c_composition_preservation_contrav.description: W^flip(pi_2 circ pi_1) = W^flip(pi_1) circ W^flip(pi_2); standard Gelfand-duality contravariant composition

axiom_d_topography_action_consistency.description: UCP restriction to operator-sub-system preserves topography action

axiom_e_cover_action_consistency.description: UCP restriction at each cutoff level preserves cover action

commutative_restriction_comparison.claim: W^flip restricted to iota(LorPLG_cov) agrees with W (A.3' Wick-rotation functor) up to natural iso
commutative_restriction_comparison.proof_method: Gelfand-Naimark identification: UCP pullback on commutative C*-algebra = continuous-function pullback = dual Gelfand spectrum map

phase1_chirality_graded_restriction.claim: W^flip restricted to Case A (M=0 enlargement) agrees with Phase-1 Theorem 1.3-Q1' W^flip,M=0

hierarchical_containment: W on commutative MS: pi^K dual on Gelfand spectrum hat(M^L)
W^flip,M=0 on chirality-graded: pi^K_flip dual on doubled spectrum hat(M^L_flip,M=0)
W^flip on full M!=0 enlarged: (pi^K_flip)* UCP pullback on state space S(M^L_flip_full)

phase_2_b3_input_specification.morphism_to_verify: pi^K_flip_k_to_k+1: truncation projector morphism between successive cutoff Krein PPQMS (natural Berezin/projection nesting)
phase_2_b3_input_specification.image_under_W_flip: W^flip(pi^K_flip_k_to_k+1) = (pi^K_flip_k_to_k+1)^* as UCP map on enlarged OSLPLS objects
axioms_to_verify_per_cell: M1: topography preservation between truncated enlarged topographies
M3: basepoint pullback omega_W^L | M_k circ (pi^*) = omega_W^L | M_k+1
M5: modular flow intertwining (automatic from M3, sanity verification at panel level)

expected_behavior.panel_residuals_match_paper45: bit-exact agreement with Paper 45 section 6 panel values Lprop(2,3) = 2.0746, Lprop(3,5) = 1.6101, Lprop(4,7) = 1.3223
expected_behavior.riemannian_limit_recovery: at N_t = 1, W^flip reduces to Riemannian-only morphism action matching Paper 38 single-factor results bit-exact
expected_behavior.no_new_numerical_content: enlarged-substrate morphism action introduces no new numerical content at functoriality level; substantive content is in strong-form propinquity bound (Phase-2.B.2)

what_phase_2_b1_establishes: Definition 2.1-B1 source morphism class (Topographic Krein M-isometry on enlarged-flip substrate)
Definition 3.1-B1 bridge functor on morphisms via algebraic pullback in contravariant direction
Theorem 1.1-B1 functoriality (5 axioms verified at theorem-grade rigor)
Theorem 5.1-B1 commutative-case restriction agreement with A.3'
Phase-2.B.3 numerical verification panel input specification
what_phase_2_b1_does_not_establish: Bridge Theorem 6.4'-Q1' substantive content B2'-Q1' off-orbit super-additivity (Phase-2.B.2)
Functor faithfulness (named open follow-on for Phase-2.B.2)
Numerical verification of morphism action (Phase-2.B.3, gated on this specification)
Naturality with respect to other bridge functors (Phase-2.B.2 or Phase-2.D)
failure_modes_avoided: Claim Phase-2.B.1 closes the Bridge Theorem - explicitly defer to Phase-2.B.2
Claim functoriality without verifying all four axioms - section 4 verifies (a)-(e) explicitly
Skip commutative-case restriction comparison - section 5 establishes Theorem 5.1-B1
Conflate source-side morphism class with target-side OSLPLS morphism class - sections 2/3/4 distinct
Promote Phase-2.B.1 from mechanical to substantive - section 6.2 recommends GO to Phase-2.B.3, parallel Phase-2.B.2

finding_1.title: Morphism action is fully constrained by the substrate (parallel to Phase-2.A object finding)
finding_1.description: Bridge functor's morphism action is dictated by (a) Phase-2.A object construction, (b) Connes-vS contravariant UCP convention, (c) source-side morphism class (extension of A.2' Def 2.3). No design freedom; the morphism action is forced. Parallels Phase-2.A's 'object is structurally constrained, not freely chosen' finding.

finding_2.title: Contravariance is the convention-bridge between covariant Krein source and contravariant UCP target
finding_2.description: Source category uses covariant Krein M-isometries (proper *-epimorphisms in natural causal direction); target uses contravariant UCP maps. Bridge functor W^flip pulls covariant source arrow back to contravariant target arrow via algebraic pullback. Natural Connes-vS convention (operator-system category presented contravariantly per Gelfand duality). A.3' handled this via Gelfand spectrum dual; Phase-2.B.1 via algebraic pullback (reduces to A.3' on commutative sub-category per Theorem 5.1-B1). Contravariance is inherited from underlying duality, not a Phase-2.B.1 design choice.
